title: Cut my risk in half and started trading less. Best decision I've made this year.
author: Independent_Gur8648
selftext:
A couple months ago I cut my position size in half and stopped forcing trades every day.

The setup hasn't changed. The biggest change is that I'm much more selective about when I trade and when I simply stay out. If something feels off, I don't need to be involved.

I've been traveling quite a bit lately, so most days I only trade when I actually have time to sit down and focus. Surprisingly, my results haven't suffered much, and my stress level is nowhere near what it used to be.

June started with two clean trading days, about +$12k so far. Nothing crazy, just sticking to the plan and taking what the market gives.

Funny enough, after a long stretch without a red day, part of me keeps expecting one to show up. But that's trading. I'd rather take a controlled loss than force opportunities that aren't there.

Current focus:Smaller size. Fewer trades. Only A+ setups. Protect capital first

The P&L is nice, but honestly the biggest win has been getting my time and peace of mind back.
